data.title: Ryan Choi, associate of Andrew Left, to pay $1.8M to settle SEC charges
data.description:
• Andrew Left associate Ryan Choi agreed to pay more than $1.8M to settle charges that he negligently engaged in a scheme that misled readers of Citron Research in connection with two tweets published by the platform, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said on Tuesday.
  • Andrew Left, who operates the Citron Research website and related platforms, was charged in July 2024 for publishing false and misleading statements regarding his supposed stock trading recommendations.
  • The SEC complaint alleged that Choi worked with Left in December 2020 on research and content for two buy recommendations that Left issued through Citron. Choi is accused of failing to conduct adequate research or due diligence that he provided to Left to support the recommendations.
  • The SEC also alleged that Choi traded on the price increases that came after the two Citron Research tweets were published and negligently failed to disclose his trading activity in the tweets. As a result, Choi made almost $1.65M in profits from the trading.
  • Without admitting or denying the allegations in the complaint, Choi agreed to pay a civil penalty of $115K, disgorgement of $1.55M, and prejudgment interest of $64.8K.

data.title: Duke Energy cut at KeyBanc, as storm response seen pressuring balance sheet
data.description:
Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) is little changed in Tuesday's trading as KeyBanc downgrades shares to Sector Weight from Overweight, believing the major catalysts for the stock have been priced in, and the recent storms may present a near-term challenge.

Following a successful multiyear restructuring process and several constructive regulatory outcomes across its core jurisdictions, Duke (DUK) is now trading at a slight premium to peers, which analyst Sophie Karp says is deserved, but she sees more upside elsewhere in the utilities space.

Duke Energy (DUK) is well positioned to benefit from load growth, driven by broader economic development as well as data center demand, across its territories, and expects to see 10K-18K GWh of load growth by 2028, but "this aspect of the story is well understood," Karp says.

While hurricanes Helene and Milton are not likely to pose a major headwind to Q3 earnings, Karp thinks the Q4 impact will be more pronounced both from lost sales and higher O&M perspective.

As a majority of storm costs will be capitalized, Karp expects the final price tag will pressure Duke's (DUK) balance sheet, just as the company closes in on achieving its 14% FFO/debt target in 2024.

"Given the fact that Duke recently received MYRP in Florida and North Carolina, we believe the ultimate recovery of these balances might not occur until after 2025, potentially putting upward pressure on Duke's $500M/year stated equity needs," according to Karp.

data.title: Humana, Cigna talks informal at best, no developments expected soon - CTFN
data.description:
Discussions between Humana (NYSE:HUM) and Cigna (CI) remain informal at best, and there are no manor developments expected before or even immediately after the US presidential election.

Humana (HUM) is under pressure as its share price dropped, though it's not rushing into any deal with Cigna (CI), according to a CTFN report on Tuesday, which cited a source familiar with the matter.

It appears that the companies are at least "testing the waters," a lawyer representing a competitor to both companies told CTFN.

The CTFN item comes after Bloomberg reported on Friday that the two managed care companies have had informal talks about a potential transaction. No decision has been made and Cigna or Humana may push any deal past the new year or could decide to abandon any deal, according to Bloomberg.

Share of Humana (HUM) edged higher by 0.7% on Tuesday, while Cigna (CI) fell 1.2%.

Humana (HUM) is set to report Q3 results on Oct. 30.

data.title: BlackRock launches two new ETFs that tackle AI and growth opportunities
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) on Tuesday bolstered its exchange-traded fund lineup with the launch of two new actively managed funds centered on technology growth and artificial intelligence (AI).

The world's largest asset manager said it believes the long-term impact of AI will be "profound" despite it still being in early stages.

“We are at the dawn of an intelligence revolution,” Tony Kim, Head of the Fundamental Equities Technology Group at BlackRock (BLK), said in a statement. “These active ETFs can help investors seize outsized and overlooked investment opportunities across the full stack of AI and advanced technologies.”

See information about the two ETFs below:

iShares A.I. Innovation and Tech Active ETF (NYSEARCA:BAI)

  • Fund Objective: Invests in a concentrated portfolio of 20-40 global AI and technology stocks across all markets.
  • Number of Holdings: 34
  • Top Three Holdings: Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT).
  • Expense Fee: 0.68%.

iShares Technology Opportunities Active ETF (NYSE:TEK)

  • Fund Objective: Invests in global technology companies across semiconductors, software, hardware, internet, services, content and infrastructure, and new industries.
  • Number of Holdings: 57
  • Top Three Holdings: Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), and Apple (AAPL).
  • Expense Fee: 0.75%.

BAI and TEK will be the latest ETFs to join several existing funds that are focused on AI and technology, including:

  • KraneShares Artificial Intelligence and Technology ETF (AGIX)
  • GX Artificial Intelligence & Tech ETF (AIQ)
  • GX Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF (BOTZ)
  • ALPS Disruptive Technologies ETF (DTEC)
  • First Trust NASDAQ Artificial Intelligence and Robotics ETF (ROBT)
  • Robo Global Artificial Intelligence ETF (THNQ)
  • Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT)

data.title: Flying air taxis are on the radar after the FAA finalizes key rules
data.description:
The FAA published a highly anticipated special federal aviation regulation that paves the way for the new generation of eVTOL aircraft.

At the National Business Aviation Administration-Business Aviation Convention and Exhibition in Las Vegas, FAA Administrator Mike Whitaker signed the rule, which contains initial operational rules and pilot training requirements for powered-lift aircraft and will remain in effect for 10 years. Whitaker said the rule recognizes air taxis as an entirely new type of aircraft that will soon join airplanes and helicopters in the sky. The aircraft take off and land vertically, like helicopters, but fly like fixed-wing planes.

Notably, Whitaker said the FAA is stressing safety as it works to fold the new aircraft into the nation's airspace. He said “powered-lift aircraft” are the first new category of aircraft in nearly 80 years, since the dawn of helicopters, and the rule will allow for their widespread operation.

Joby Aviation (NYSE:JOBY) CEO JoeBen Bevirt said the rules will ensure the U.S. continues to play a global leadership role in the development and adoption of clean flight. The FAA development has broad implications because airlines see air taxis as a way to deliver passengers to airports. Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) said in 2022 it would invest $60 million in Joby, and this month Toyota Motor (NYSE:TM) announced a $500 million investment. United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) is backing California-based company Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) with an order for 200 aircraft that Archer said could be worth $1 billion, with an option for $500 million more.

data.title: Fluor raised to Buy at Citi on improved execution, growing backlog
data.description:
Fluor (NYSE:FLR) hovers near the flatline in Tuesday's trading even as Citi upgrades shares to Buy from Neutral with a $65 price target, raised from $52, saying the company's outlook and execution "continues to evolve positively," while the backlog has increased over the last several quarters.

Citi analyst Andrew Kaplowitz says his upgrade is mostly focused on the longer term, believing Fluor's (FLR) book-to-bill for the next few quarters could be less than 1x - which historically would not bode well for the stock - but should pick back up over 1x likely toward the middle of 2025.

However, the stock "could still react reasonably well" in the meantime, given Fluor's (FLR) improved execution and relative valuation discount vs. the S&P 500, according to Kaplowitz.

The stock is not pricing in the potential for Fluor (FLR) to monetize at least several hundred million dollars from its ~50% equity stake in Nuscale Power (SMR), the analyst adds.

data.title: Goldman Sachs said to face CFPB penalty related to Apple credit-card program
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) will face a penalty of tens of millions of dollars from the U.S. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for its handling of its credit card operations, according to a Tuesday media report.

The CFPB's enforcement action is linked to a probe into Goldman's (GS) customer service practices, particularly concerning its Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) credit-card program, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

In late 2023, Apple (AAPL) had tapped out on its consumer-finance partnership with Goldman (GS), including exiting their credit-card and savings-account contract. The Journal reported last month that JPMorgan Chase (JPM) was in talks with Apple about assuming the iPhone maker's card program from Goldman, which is already racking up losses from its costly consumer-sector exit.

The consumer watchdog's enforcement action is expected to be announced this week, with total penalties, including customer reimbursements, potentially exceeding $50M, the WSJ reported.

data.title: Polls show support for marijuana legalization in red states
data.description:
Recent polls from two states considered red because of the political slant of its electorate towards Republicans indicate a significant percentage of voters there support legalizing marijuana for recreational use.

A recent survey conducted by Midwest Newsroom and Emerson College Polling found that 56% of Kansas voters back legalizing cannabis for recreational use and 72% back medical marijuana. Cannabis is currently completely illegal in the state.

In Utah, where medical marijuana is legal, support for adult-use marijuana isn't as strong. A poll commissioned by the Utah Patients Coalition and conducted by Noble Predictive Insights found 50% support legalizing recreational cannabis. Other results showed 38% only support medical cannabis, and 9% responded marijuana should be illegal.

Marijuana will be on the ballot in several other states in November, including Florida and Arkansas. While a recent poll in Arkansas showed a majority of voters support a ballot initiative to expand the state's medical marijuana program, a state Supreme Court decision on Oct. 21 ruled that those votes will not count, Marijuana Moment reported.

In Florida, which permits medical marijuana, voters will cast ballots on a proposed amendment to allow adult-use marijuana.

Multi-state operators: Ascend Wellness (OTCQX:AAWH), Curaleaf Holdings (OTCPK:CURLF), Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF), Green Thumb Industries (OTCQX:GTBIF), Trulieve Cannabis (OTCQX:TCNNF), Ayr Wellness (OTCQX:AYRWF), Verano Holdings (OTCQX:VRNOF), and Jushi Holdings (OTCQX:JUSHF).

Cannabis ETFs: AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (YOLO), Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (NYSEARCA:MJ), and Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (CNBS).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

Readers interested in investing topics tied to the upcoming election can find more information at the upcoming Seeking Alpha Investing Forum: Election 2024; please visit the event website.

data.title: NVR stock dips as decline in new orders weighs on Q3 results
data.description:
NVR (NYSE:NVR) shares drifted down 2.2% in Tuesday midafternoon trading after the homebuilder posted Q3 earnings that fell short of the average analyst estimate as new orders retreated during the three-month period, suggesting a potential slowdown in demand for new homes.

Q3 GAAP EPS of $130.50, trailing the $131.80 consensus, rose from $120.69 in the previous quarter and $125.26 in Q3 2023.

Revenue of $2.73B, surpassing the $2.70B expected, climbed from $2.55B in Q2 and $2.57B in Q3 of last year.

New orders fell to 5,650 units from 6,067 in the prior quarter and advanced from 4,746 a year before. The average sales price of new orders was $450.7K, down from $458.8K in Q2 and -1% from the year-earlier period.

The cancellation rate edged up to 15% from 13% in Q2 and 14% in Q3 2023.

As of Sept. 30, NVR's (NVR) backlog of home sold but not settled increased on a unit basis by 9% to 11,339 units and increased on a dollar basis by 11% to $5.32 billion when compared to the respective backlog unit and dollar balances as of Sept. 30, 2023.

data.title: Novo's Rybelsus results may not be clinically meaningful, Bernstein says
data.description:
Bernstein said Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) recently released SOUL study results for Rybelsus in the reduction of major cardiovascular events in diabetic patients were modest and not as robust as results from the SELECT study for Wegovy.

In a note released Tuesday, Bernstein noted that the SOUL study showed a 14% relative reduction in the co-primary endpoints of cardiovascular death, non-fatal myocardial infarction and stroke in patients who took Rybelsus versus placebo.

Bernstein added, however, that the benefit should be at least greater than 15% to be clinically meaningful. It pointed out that the SELECT trial showed Wegovy was able to lower the risk of major cardiovascular events by 20% in obese patients.

Both Rybelsus and Wegovy contain the drug semaglutide. Rybelsus is an oral formulation of the drug and marketed for the treatment of diabetes, while Wegovy is an injected version marketed for weight loss. Another injected version of the drug, Ozempic, is also marketed for diabetes.

Bernstein said the SOUL results were more in line with Novo’s LEADER study of Victoza, also known as liraglutide, which showed a roughly 13% reduction in risk. It added that the product has never really benefitted from the results of the trial, despite a labeling update.

“We reiterate our cautious view on Novo due to already-high market expectations on diabetes and obesity franchises given the increasingly competitive landscape in obesity and pricing pressure on semaglutide from IRA,” said Bernstein, which has an underperform rating on the stock.

Novo Nordisk released data from the SOUL Rybelsus study on Monday.
